---
title: "Considerations for Illinois SGP Analyses"
subtitle: "During the Transition from SAT/PSAT to ACT/PreACT"
author:
  - name: "Damian W. Betebenner"
    affiliation: "National Center for the Improvement of Educational Assessment"
date: 2026-05-21
date-format: "[Illinois TAC Meeting · ]MMMM D, YYYY"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, styles/presentation-theme.scss]
    slide-number: true
    transition: fade
    transition-speed: fast
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    margin: 0.06
    center: false
    hash: true
    history: true
    controls: true
    progress: true
    html-math-method: mathml
    fig-align: center
    embed-resources: true
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          <link rel="preload" href="assets/fonts/NotoSansMath.woff2" as="font" type="font/woff2" crossorigin>
          <style>
            @font-face {
              font-family: "Noto Sans Math";
              src: url("assets/fonts/NotoSansMath.woff2") format("woff2");
              font-display: swap;
            }
          </style>
    include-after-body:
      - text: |
          <script src="styles/presentation-figure-modal.js"></script>
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

## {.center}

::: {style="text-align: center; padding-top: 2em;"}

[Illinois SGP Analyses]{style="font-family: 'Josefin Sans'; font-size: 1.0em; font-weight: 300; color: #7c7c7c; display: block; margin-bottom: 0.5em; letter-spacing: 0.18em; text-transform: uppercase;"}

[Considerations During the Transition to ACT]{style="font-family: 'Josefin Sans'; font-size: 2.2em; font-weight: 700; color: #3498DB; display: block; margin-bottom: 0.5em;"}

[Cohort- and Baseline-Referenced SGPs Across the SAT/PSAT → ACT/PreACT Vendor Change]{style="font-family: 'Josefin Sans'; font-size: 1.0em; font-weight: 300; color: #7c7c7c; font-style: italic; display: block; margin-bottom: 1.5em;"}

**Damian W. Betebenner**<br>
National Center for the Improvement of Educational Assessment<br>
<dbetebenner@nciea.org>

[Illinois TAC · May 21, 2026]{.conference-badge style="display: block; margin-top: 1.2em;"}

:::


## Roadmap {.smaller}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

**Background**

- The current Illinois growth workflow
- The 2025 high-school assessment transition
- Two simultaneous policy changes to keep separate

**The SGP Distinction**

- Cohort-referenced vs baseline-referenced SGPs
- Why baseline matrices do not travel across vendors

**Cohort-Referenced Transition Map**

- 2025 · 2026 · 2027 grade-9/10/11 patterns
- Recurring vs transitional sequences

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

**Baseline-Referenced Implications**

- Calibration timing by grade span
- Three baseline design choices

**Performance-Level Reset**

- What is and is not affected

**Implementation 2026–2027**

- Configuration, knots, SIMEX, linking

**Recommendations & Decisions**

- Reporting approach, ISBE decision points
- Bottom line and timing roadmap

:::
:::


## Purpose

This memorandum lays out **technical and reporting considerations** for Illinois SGP analyses in ELA and mathematics for grades 4 through 11 during the transition from the **SAT/PSAT** high-school system to the **ACT/PreACT** high-school system.

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

**The operational picture**

- Grades 4–8 IAR analyses can continue under the established cohort + baseline workflow
- High school enters a transition period beginning **spring 2025**
- Grade 11: ACT with Writing
- Grade 10: PreACT Secure
- Grade 9: PreACT 9 Secure

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

**The technical question**

The discussion distinguishes throughout between **cohort-referenced** and **baseline-referenced** SGPs because the two approaches are affected very differently by the vendor transition.

::: {.key-insight}
**Cohort-referenced SGPs survive the transition with caveats. Baseline-referenced high-school SGPs probably require new ACT-era coefficient matrices before they can be reported operationally (equating NEW scale to OLD can possibly be used as a workaround).**
:::

:::
:::


## {.center}

::: {style="text-align: center; padding-top: 3em;"}

[Background]{style="font-family: 'Josefin Sans'; font-size: 1.0em; font-weight: 300; color: #7c7c7c; display: block; margin-bottom: 0.6em; letter-spacing: 0.18em; text-transform: uppercase;"}

[The Transition]{style="font-family: 'Josefin Sans'; font-size: 2.4em; font-weight: 700; color: #3498DB; display: block; margin-bottom: 0.5em;"}

[What changed, when, and why both vendor and policy changes are in play.]{style="font-family: 'Josefin Sans'; font-size: 1.1em; font-weight: 300; color: #7c7c7c; font-style: italic; display: block;"}

:::


## The Current Illinois Growth Workflow

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}

**Grades 4–8 IAR — established and stable.**

- Current production script (`PARCC_IL_SGP_2024_2025.2.R`) loads three panel years (2022–2023, 2023–2024, 2024–2025)
- Configures ELA and mathematics for grade sequences:

```text
3-4
3-4-5
4-5-6
5-6-7
6-7-8
```

- Runs `abcSGP` requesting:
    - `sgp.percentiles` (cohort-referenced)
    - `sgp.percentiles.baseline` (baseline-referenced)
    - `sgp.projections` and `sgp.projections.lagged`
    - `sgp.projections.baseline` and `sgp.projections.lagged.baseline`
    - **SIMEX** measurement-error correction

:::
::: {.column width="45%"}

**What this means going into the transition**

- Grades 4–8 stay on rails: **same scale family, same baseline matrices, same projection workflow**.
- The interesting questions live entirely in **grades 9–11**.

::: {.key-insight}
**The grade-band split is the cleanest organizing principle for the rest of the discussion.** Grades 4–8 are an IAR-to-IAR problem; grades 9–11 are a vendor-transition problem.
:::

:::
:::


## The Spring 2025 High-School Vendor Change {.smaller}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

**Before (through 2024)**

| Grade | Operational assessment |
|:-----:|:----------------------|
| 11 | SAT with Essay |
| 10 | PSAT 10 |
| 9 | PSAT 8/9 |

**Beginning spring 2025**

| Grade | Operational assessment |
|:-----:|:----------------------|
| 11 | ACT with Writing |
| 10 | PreACT Secure |
| 9 | PreACT 9 Secure |

The 2026 Pre-ID documentation continues the ACT/PreACT structure for grades 9, 10, and 11.

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

**Why this is not just a cosmetic change**

- The **scale changes** by grade — see next slide
- The **conditional reference distribution** changes year over year as ACT-family priors gradually replace College Board priors
- The **first year** in which a grade-11 student has an ACT-family current score and *both* priors in the ACT family is **2027**

::: {.key-insight}
**2027 is the calibration anchor for any future ACT-era grade-11 baseline matrices.**
:::

:::
:::


## ACT-Family Scales by Grade

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}

| Grade | Assessment | Scale |
|:-----:|:------|:-----:|
| 9 | PreACT 9 Secure | 1–32 |
| 10 | PreACT Secure | 1–35 |
| 11 | ACT with Writing | 1–36 |

**Implications for the SGP workflow**

- SGP estimation does **not** require a vertical scale across grades — conditional percentiles are well-defined within grade
- It **does** require correct, grade-specific knots and boundaries on each current-score scale
- ELA and mathematics score distributions need direct validation before production

:::
::: {.column width="45%"}

::: {.key-insight}
**The grade-specific scales do not prevent SGP calculation.** They do require careful, content- and grade-specific scale validation in `SGPstateData[["IL"]]` before any production run.
:::

The 2025 production script already shows the right pattern in its science knots/boundaries block — that pattern needs to be extended to ELA and mathematics on the ACT-family scales.

:::
:::


## {.center}

::: {style="text-align: center; padding-top: 3em;"}

[The SGP Distinction]{style="font-family: 'Josefin Sans'; font-size: 1.0em; font-weight: 300; color: #7c7c7c; display: block; margin-bottom: 0.6em; letter-spacing: 0.18em; text-transform: uppercase;"}

[What Kind of SGP?]{style="font-family: 'Josefin Sans'; font-size: 2.4em; font-weight: 700; color: #3498DB; display: block; margin-bottom: 0.5em;"}

[Cohort-referenced and baseline-referenced SGPs answer different questions.]{style="font-family: 'Josefin Sans'; font-size: 1.1em; font-weight: 300; color: #7c7c7c; font-style: italic; display: block;"}

:::


## What Is an SGP?

::: {.theorem-box}
::: {.theorem-box-title}
Student Growth Percentile
:::

Let $Y_{g,t}$ denote a student's current-year scale score in grade $g$ and year $t$, and let $\mathbf{H}_{g,t}$ denote that student's available prior achievement history. An SGP is a percentile rank of the current score within an estimated conditional distribution:

$$\mathrm{SGP}_{g,t} = \widehat{F}_{Y_{g,t}\mid \mathbf{H}_{g,t}}(Y_{g,t}\mid \mathbf{H}_{g,t})$$
:::

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

**The critical distinction is the reference distribution used for $\widehat{F}$.**

- **Cohort-referenced:** $\widehat{F}$ estimated from the *current operational cohort*
- **Baseline-referenced:** $\widehat{F}$ fixed by saved coefficient matrices from a *historical reference group*

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

::: {.key-insight}
**Same percentile machinery, different reference distribution — and that single difference is what makes the vendor transition affect the two SGP types so differently.**
:::

:::
:::


## Cohort-Referenced SGPs {.smaller}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}

**The question answered**

> Among students in *this year's* statewide cohort with similar prior achievement histories, where does this student's current achievement fall?

**Why this survives the vendor transition**

- The current cohort *itself* supplies the conditional reference distribution
- No vertical scale is required across grades or vendors
- Prior IAR, PSAT, or SAT scores can be used as conditioning variables alongside current ACT/PreACT scores
- 2025 is the first ACT-family year; the cohort SGP describes a student's standing relative to **other 2025 grade-11 students with similar PSAT 10 and PSAT 8/9 histories**

:::
::: {.column width="45%"}

::: {.key-insight}
**For transition years, cohort-referenced SGPs are the most defensible operational growth metric.**
:::

**The catch**

The transition is *part of the estimand*. A 2025 grade-11 cohort SGP is not directly comparable to a future 2028 grade-11 cohort SGP whose reference cohort has fully ACT-family priors. Public-facing language should consider labeling transition-year cohort SGPs as such.

:::
:::


## Baseline-Referenced SGPs {.smaller}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}

**The question answered**

> Where does this student's current achievement fall relative to the **fixed baseline distribution** for students with similar prior achievement histories?

**How it is implemented**

A baseline SGP is anchored to **saved coefficient matrices** for a specific combination of:

- state · content area · grade sequence
- score scale · knots and boundaries
- measurement-error correction design

::: {.key-insight}
**Baseline SGPs are valuable only when the saved matrices correspond to the same operational assessment design being used for current students.**
:::

:::
::: {.column width="45%"}

**Why IAR baseline matrices do not transfer to ACT/PreACT**

- Different score scale (ACT 1–36 vs IAR scale)
- Different prior-score family (College Board / IAR vs ACT family)
- Different score precision and CSEM definitions
- Different conditional distribution shape

::: {.key-insight-box}
::: {.key-insight-box-title}
Category Error
:::
**Applying IAR baseline matrices to ACT/PreACT current scores would be a category error.** Applying transition-year matrices to later stable ACT-era sequences would also be problematic if the current/prior assessment pattern differs.
:::

:::
:::


## {.center}

::: {style="text-align: center; padding-top: 3em;"}

[Cohort-Referenced SGPs]{style="font-family: 'Josefin Sans'; font-size: 1.0em; font-weight: 300; color: #7c7c7c; display: block; margin-bottom: 0.6em; letter-spacing: 0.18em; text-transform: uppercase;"}

[The Transition Map]{style="font-family: 'Josefin Sans'; font-size: 2.4em; font-weight: 700; color: #3498DB; display: block; margin-bottom: 0.5em;"}

[Year-by-year, grade-by-grade — what's current, what's prior, and what it means.]{style="font-family: 'Josefin Sans'; font-size: 1.1em; font-weight: 300; color: #7c7c7c; font-style: italic; display: block;"}

:::


## 2025 — First ACT-Family Year {.smaller}

| Grade | Current | First prior | Second prior |
|:-----:|:--------|:------------|:-------------|
| 9  | PreACT 9 Secure   | 2024 IAR grade 8        | 2023 IAR grade 7 |
| 10 | PreACT Secure     | 2024 PSAT 8/9 grade 9   | 2023 IAR grade 8 |
| 11 | ACT with Writing  | 2024 PSAT 10 grade 10   | 2023 PSAT 8/9 grade 9 |

**Reading the row**

- **Grade 9** is the *recurring* IAR-to-PreACT-9-Secure pattern that we will see every year going forward
- **Grade 10** and **grade 11** are *transition* sequences with College Board priors
- All three current scores are ACT-family for the first time

::: {.key-insight}
**2025 cohort-referenced HS SGPs are technically feasible — but they describe a one-of-a-kind cohort with mixed-vendor priors. They should be reported with explicit transition-year labeling.**
:::


## 2026 — ACT-Family Current and First Prior {.smaller}

| Grade | Current | First prior | Second prior |
|:-----:|:--------|:------------|:-------------|
| 9  | PreACT 9 Secure  | 2025 IAR grade 8           | 2024 IAR grade 7 |
| 10 | PreACT Secure    | 2025 PreACT 9 Secure gr 9  | 2024 IAR grade 8 |
| 11 | ACT with Writing | 2025 PreACT Secure gr 10   | 2024 PSAT 8/9 grade 9 |

**Reading the row**

- **Grade 10** is now ACT-family in current and first prior; second prior is grade 8 IAR — this is the **recurring** grade-10 sequence
- **Grade 11** is ACT-family in current and first prior, but the second prior is **still PSAT 8/9** — a one-prior-residual transition year
- A one-prior 2026 grade-11 model would be ACT-family throughout — but would not be directly comparable to a two-prior model

::: {.key-insight}
**2026 is the year the grade-11 model decision matters most: two priors keeps the College Board residual; one prior is fully ACT-family but a different model.**
:::


## 2027 — First Full ACT-Family Grade-11 Sequence {.smaller}

| Grade | Current | First prior | Second prior |
|:-----:|:--------|:------------|:-------------|
| 9  | PreACT 9 Secure  | 2026 IAR grade 8           | 2025 IAR grade 7 |
| 10 | PreACT Secure    | 2026 PreACT 9 Secure gr 9  | 2025 IAR grade 8 |
| 11 | ACT with Writing | 2026 PreACT Secure gr 10   | 2025 PreACT 9 Secure gr 9 |

**Reading the row**

- **All three current scores and all priors** are in the ACT family for the first time
- This is the first natural calibration year for a **two-prior ACT-era grade-11 baseline**
- Grades 9 and 10 are on their recurring IAR-to-ACT-family sequences

::: {.key-insight}
**2027 is the calibration anchor.** Operational use of a full grade-11 ACT-era baseline should not occur before **2028**, after technical review of the calibration year.
:::


## Implications by Grade Band {.smaller}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

**Grades 4–8 (IAR)**

- 2026 and later remain IAR-to-IAR analyses
- Continue the standard grade sequences `3-4`, `3-4-5`, `4-5-6`, `5-6-7`, `6-7-8`
- Cohort + baseline workflows both stable

**Grade 9**

- IAR-to-PreACT-9-Secure is a **recurring** grade-span transition, not a temporary vendor transition
- Grade 9 SGPs will *always* condition on middle-school IAR history under the current rule
- A separate prior-data rule would change this

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

**Grade 10**

- 2026 is the first year with ACT-family current and first-prior scores
- Second prior is grade 8 IAR — part of the **recurring** grade-10 sequence
- 2026 is therefore the natural first candidate calibration year for a recurring grade-10 ACT-era baseline

**Grade 11**

- 2026: ACT-family current and first prior; PSAT 8/9 still in the second-prior slot under a two-prior model
- **2027:** ACT with Writing, PreACT Secure, PreACT 9 Secure all present in the same longitudinal sequence

:::
:::


## Recurring vs Transitional Sequences {.smaller}

A useful organizing distinction across the three transition years:

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}

**Recurring sequences** — will repeat every year going forward

- Grades 4–8 IAR-to-IAR (no change)
- Grade 9: IAR grades 7, 8 → PreACT 9 Secure
- Grade 10 (from 2026): IAR grade 8 → PreACT 9 Secure → PreACT Secure
- Grade 11 (from 2027): PreACT 9 Secure → PreACT Secure → ACT with Writing

**Transitional sequences** — appear only during the vendor changeover

- Grade 10 in 2025: PSAT 8/9 → PreACT Secure
- Grade 11 in 2025–2026: PSAT 8/9 / PSAT 10 → ACT with Writing

:::
::: {.column width="45%"}

::: {.key-insight}
**Calibration follows the recurring sequences, not the transitional ones.** Transitional years can be reported cohort-referenced; baseline calibration should anchor to the *first stable year* for each recurring sequence.
:::

This is why grade-9 and grade-10 baseline calibration could become possible earlier than grade-11 baseline calibration — they reach a stable recurring sequence sooner.

:::
:::


## {.center}

::: {style="text-align: center; padding-top: 3em;"}

[Baseline-Referenced SGPs]{style="font-family: 'Josefin Sans'; font-size: 1.0em; font-weight: 300; color: #7c7c7c; display: block; margin-bottom: 0.6em; letter-spacing: 0.18em; text-transform: uppercase;"}

[A Separate Design Problem]{style="font-family: 'Josefin Sans'; font-size: 2.4em; font-weight: 700; color: #3498DB; display: block; margin-bottom: 0.5em;"}

[Calibration timing, design choices, and the operational default during transition.]{style="font-family: 'Josefin Sans'; font-size: 1.1em; font-weight: 300; color: #7c7c7c; font-style: italic; display: block;"}

:::


## Calibration Timing by Grade Span {.smaller}

| Grade span | Recurring assessment sequence | First stable calibration year | Baseline recommendation |
|:-----------|:------------------------------|:------------------------------|:------------------------|
| Grades 4–8 | IAR current with IAR priors | Already available | Continue cohort + baseline; update projections to new benchmarks |
| Grade 9 | PreACT 9 Secure with IAR gr 8 (and gr 7) priors | 2025, but also first ACT-family year | Cohort during transition; ACT-era baseline only after 2025–2027 distributions show stability |
| Grade 10 | PreACT Secure with PreACT 9 Secure (gr 9) and IAR gr 8 priors | **2026** | Calibrate from 2026 (or pooled 2026–2027); operational use no earlier than 2027 |
| Grade 11 | ACT with Writing with PreACT Secure (gr 10) and PreACT 9 Secure (gr 9) priors | **2027** | Full two-prior baseline not operational before **2028**; one-prior 2026 baseline only as a distinct, explicitly labeled model |

::: {.key-insight}
**Different grades become stable in different years.** A pooled or grade-specific calibration design is therefore unavoidable.
:::


## Three Baseline Design Choices {.smaller}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="33%"}

**Single baseline year**

- Easiest to explain
- Closest to the current fixed-baseline interpretation
- **Risk:** sensitive to first-year administration effects if the chosen year is too early
- Defensible only after technical evaluation of distributional stability

:::
::: {.column width="33%"}

**Pooled baseline period**

- Pool 2026–2027, or 2025–2027 where the assessment sequence is appropriate
- **Stabilizes** coefficient estimation
- Pooling design must be **grade-specific** because grades reach stability in different years
- Stronger fixed-reference interpretation than rolling

:::
::: {.column width="33%"}

**Rolling reference**

- Improves recency
- **Weakens** the fixed-reference interpretation
- Better understood as a *smoothed cohort reference* than a traditional baseline
- Reasonable as a transitional reporting choice — less clearly a "baseline"

:::
:::

::: {.key-insight}
**The choice is not just statistical — it is interpretive.** Each option implies a different answer to "what does a 50 mean?"
:::


## Default Recommendation Until ACT-Era Matrices Exist {.smaller}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}

**For accountability reporting in 2026 and 2027:**

1. **Grades 4–8:** Continue cohort + baseline-referenced ELA and mathematics SGPs using the established IAR workflow and existing Illinois baseline matrices.

2. **Grades 9, 10, 11:** Report **only cohort-referenced** SGPs.

3. Do **not** report baseline-referenced HS SGPs until ACT-era matrices have been:
   - **created** for the recurring grade sequences
   - **reviewed** for distributional stability
   - **approved** for the intended accountability use

:::
::: {.column width="45%"}

::: {.key-insight}
**Defensible default: keep the grades 4–8 baseline workflow, add cohort-only HS analyses with explicit transition labels, and defer HS baseline reporting until ACT-era matrices are deliberately calibrated.**
:::

This is the same posture the 2025 production script already supports — it requests baseline outputs only for the IAR grade sequences. Extending to HS only requires adding cohort-referenced grade-9, grade-10, and grade-11 sequences without enabling baseline flags.

:::
:::


## SIMEX, Linking, and Participation {.smaller}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

**SIMEX measurement-error correction**

- The 2025 script already requests both uncorrected and SIMEX-corrected SGPs
- For grades 4–8: continue if IAR score and CSEM fields remain consistent
- For HS: SIMEX requires defensible CSEM values for ACT/PreACT score variables
- If ACT/PreACT CSEM values are unavailable or not comparable to IAR CSEM, **run uncorrected cohort SGPs** for HS or complete a separate technical evaluation first

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

**Student linking and participation rules**

- Vendor systems, Pre-ID processes, and assessment records all changed during the transition
- The 2025 script already contains an Illinois-specific data-cleaning step that renames student identifier fields in prior-year files — audit this across **ACT/PreACT, PSAT/SAT, and IAR** files
- 2026 Pre-ID: SIS enrollment auto-includes students except where IDEA + DLM-AA = Yes
- For growth, the question is **operational accountability score validity**, not college reportability

::: {.key-insight}
**Three independent risk surfaces — SIMEX, linking, and participation rules — each warrant a discrete go/no-go check before HS production.**
:::

:::
:::


## {.center}

::: {style="text-align: center; padding-top: 3em;"}

[Recommendations]{style="font-family: 'Josefin Sans'; font-size: 1.0em; font-weight: 300; color: #7c7c7c; display: block; margin-bottom: 0.6em; letter-spacing: 0.18em; text-transform: uppercase;"}

[What to Report, How to Label It]{style="font-family: 'Josefin Sans'; font-size: 2.4em; font-weight: 700; color: #3498DB; display: block; margin-bottom: 0.5em;"}

[An operational posture for 2026 reporting and the decisions Illinois needs to make.]{style="font-family: 'Josefin Sans'; font-size: 1.1em; font-weight: 300; color: #7c7c7c; font-style: italic; display: block;"}

:::


## Recommended Reporting Approach for 2026 {.smaller}

::: {style="font-size: 0.95em;"}

1. **Grades 4–8.** Continue cohort- and baseline-referenced ELA and mathematics SGPs using the established IAR workflow and existing Illinois baseline coefficient matrices. Update projections and growth-to-standard targets to the new proficiency benchmarks.

2. **Grade 9.** Report cohort-referenced ELA and mathematics SGPs using PreACT 9 Secure current scores and available IAR grade 8 and grade 7 prior scores. Treat this as the recurring IAR-to-HS sequence. **Do not report baseline-referenced grade 9 SGPs** until an ACT-era grade 9 baseline has been evaluated.

3. **Grade 10.** Report cohort-referenced ELA and mathematics SGPs using PreACT Secure current, PreACT 9 Secure first-prior, and IAR grade 8 second-prior scores where available. Consider 2026 as the **first candidate calibration year** for a recurring grade-10 ACT-era baseline.

4. **Grade 11.** Report cohort-referenced ELA and mathematics SGPs using ACT with Writing current and PreACT Secure first-prior scores. If two priors are used, document that the 2026 second prior is PSAT 8/9. **Defer full two-prior baseline-referenced grade 11 SGPs** until after 2027 calibration and validation.

5. **Public interpretation.** Label 2025 and 2026 high-school SGPs as **transition-year cohort-referenced** growth measures. Avoid presenting them as baseline-referenced or as directly comparable to future ACT-era baseline results.

:::


## Suggested Public-Interpretation Language

::: {.key-insight}

High school SGPs are **cohort-referenced** measures calculated from the current year's statewide assessment results and available prior state assessment scores.

Because Illinois transitioned from SAT/PSAT to ACT/PreACT beginning in spring 2025, high school SGPs for 2025 and some 2026 grade sequences reflect **transition-year assessment histories**.

These results describe student growth relative to academic peers in the **same statewide cohort**; they are *not* baseline-referenced SGPs unless explicitly identified as such.

:::

::: {style="text-align: center; margin-top: 1.2em;"}
*Suggested verbatim language for ISBE public reporting during 2025 and 2026 high-school growth releases.*
:::


## Bottom Line — Timing Roadmap {.smaller}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}

**Year-by-year operational picture**

| Year | Grades 4–8 | Grades 9–11 |
|:----:|:-----------|:------------|
| 2025 | Cohort + baseline (IAR) | Cohort only — first ACT-family year |
| 2026 | Cohort + baseline (IAR) | Cohort only; 2026 candidate calibration for grade 10 |
| 2027 | Cohort + baseline (IAR) | Cohort only; **first full ACT-family grade-11 sequence** — calibration anchor |
| 2028 | Cohort + baseline (IAR) | Earliest prudent operational use of a **two-prior grade-11 ACT-era baseline** |

:::
::: {.column width="45%"}

::: {.key-insight}
**The ACT transition does not prevent Illinois from calculating high-school cohort-referenced SGPs.** It does prevent a simple extension of the existing baseline-referenced IAR workflow to high school.
:::

**The strongest technical course**

- Continue grades 4–8 cohort + baseline analyses (with updated baseline matrices)
- Add cohort-referenced HS analyses with explicit transition labels
- Delay HS baseline-referenced reporting until ACT-era matrices are calibrated, validated, and approved
- Under a two-prior model, **2028** is the earliest prudent year for operational HS baseline use

:::
:::


## {.center}

::: {style="text-align: center; padding-top: 2em;"}

[Considerations for Illinois SGP Analyses]{style="font-family: 'Josefin Sans'; font-size: 1.6em; font-weight: 700; display: block; margin-bottom: 0.3em;"}

[During the Transition from SAT/PSAT to ACT/PreACT]{style="font-family: 'Josefin Sans'; font-size: 0.9em; font-weight: 300; color: #7c7c7c; display: block; margin-bottom: 2em;"}

**Damian W. Betebenner**<br>
National Center for the Improvement of Educational Assessment<br>
<dbetebenner@nciea.org>

[Thank you · Discussion]{style="font-family: 'Josefin Sans'; font-size: 1.3em; font-weight: 600; color: #3498DB; display: block; margin-top: 1.5em;"}

[Illinois TAC · May 21, 2026]{.conference-badge style="display: block; margin-top: 1em;"}

:::
